source_contract: ScaleFlag is derived from the caller flag, shared with measurement and drawing, then cleared after rendering.
proof_input: RE-165 PrintString families with alignment and scale-related state fields.
next_probe: prove scale lifetime and per-call isolation before source or marker changes

source_contract: Blink/composite flags can suppress visible rendering for a frame based on the global frame counter.
next_probe: prove frame counter timing and flag semantics in the comparison gate

source_contract: Initial and newline alignment depend on measured width plus optional top and bottom bounds.
next_probe: prove alignment arithmetic and newline bounds state symbolically

source_contract: Rendering chooses glyph definitions from font tables and forwards them to DrawChar.
proof_input: RE-165 text-source families that reach glyph drawing.
next_probe: prove glyph table identity and special-control character handling

source_contract: String measurement reads ScaleFlag and changes accumulated width for spaces and glyph metrics.
proof_input: RE-165 GetStringLength families and PrintString internal measurements.
next_probe: prove measurement uses the same scale state as rendering

source_contract: String measurement derives width and vertical bounds from font and accent metric tables.
next_probe: prove font metric table identity and accent behavior

source_contract: Optional output pointers receive top and bottom bounds only when provided by the caller.
next_probe: prove nullable output pointer behavior before changing callers or markers

source_contract: Spaces, tabs, newlines, and control characters alter measurement flow without emitting draw primitives.
proof_input: RE-165 GetStringLength caller-string and string-wad measurement families.
next_probe: prove control-character flow before equivalence readiness can rise

source_contract: Glyph drawing consumes the shared draw buffer, ordering table, font shades, and scale state.
proof_input: RE-164 visible side effects and RE-165 PrintString glyph families.
next_probe: prove draw-buffer side effects and ordering-table insertion in the comparison gate
